title.en: Biographies of Qu Yuan and Master Jia
title.raw: 《屈原賈生列傳》 Biographies of Qu Yuan and Master Jia

sentences.zh: 屈原者，名平，楚之同姓也。

translations.literal: Qu Yuan, named Ping, Chu same clan.
translations.idiomatic: Qu Yuan, whose given name was Ping, shared the royal surname of the state of Chu.

sentences.zh: 為楚懷王左徒。

translations.literal: Served Chu Huai Wang Left Tutor.
translations.idiomatic: He served as Left Minister to King Huai of Chu.

sentences.zh: 博聞彊志，明於治亂，嫻于辭令。

translations.literal: Broad learning strong will, clear on order chaos, skilled in rhetoric.
translations.idiomatic: He was widely learned with a powerful memory, well versed in the principles of governance and disorder, and eloquent in speech and composition.

sentences.zh: 入則與王圖議國事，以出號令；

translations.literal: Enter then with king plan discuss state affairs, to issue commands;
translations.idiomatic: At court, he deliberated affairs of state with the king and drafted royal edicts;

sentences.zh: 出則接遇賓客，應對諸侯。

translations.literal: Exit then receive guests, respond to feudal lords.
translations.idiomatic: abroad, he received envoys and conducted diplomacy with the feudal lords.

sentences.zh: 王甚任之。

translations.literal: King greatly relied on him.
translations.idiomatic: The king placed great trust in him.

translations.literal: Qu Yuan, named Ping, was of Chu's royal clan. He served as Chu King Huai's left aide. Broad in learning and strong in will, clear on order and chaos, skilled in rhetoric. When in court he consulted with the king on state affairs and issued commands; When abroad he received guests and responded to feudal lords. The king relied on him greatly.

sentences.zh: 上官大夫與之同列，爭寵而心害其能。

translations.literal: Shangguan Chamberlain with him same rank, competed favor yet heart resented his ability.
translations.idiomatic: Grand Master Shangguan, who held the same rank, vied with him for the king's favor and secretly resented his abilities.

sentences.zh: 懷王使屈原造為憲令，屈平屬草槁未定。

translations.literal: Huai Wang made Qu Yuan draft constitutional laws, Qu Ping had just finished draft not yet decided.
translations.idiomatic: King Huai charged Qu Yuan with drafting a new code of laws. Qu Ping had composed a rough draft but had not yet finalized it.

sentences.zh: 上官大夫見而欲奪之，屈平不與，因讒之曰：「王使屈平為令，眾莫不知，每一令出，平伐其功，（曰）以為『非我莫能為』也。」

translations.literal: Shangguan Chamberlain saw and wished seize it, Qu Ping not give, therefore slandered said: 'King made Qu Ping for laws, crowd none not know, every time law out, Ping boasted merit, said 'only I can do'.'
translations.idiomatic: Grand Master Shangguan saw the draft and wished to claim it for himself. When Qu Ping refused to hand it over, Shangguan slandered him before the king, saying: 'Your Majesty commissioned Qu Ping to draft these laws, and everyone knows it. Yet each time a new decree is issued, Ping boasts of the achievement, claiming that no one but he could have done it.'

sentences.zh: 王怒而疏屈平。

translations.literal: King angered distanced Qu Ping.
translations.idiomatic: The king grew angry and distanced himself from Qu Ping.

translations.literal: The Grand Chamberlain shared his rank, competed for favor and secretly envied his talent. King Huai ordered Qu Yuan to draft the constitution. Qu Ping had just finished the rough draft. The Grand Chamberlain saw it and wanted to steal it. Qu Ping would not give it. Thus he slandered saying: 'The king ordered Qu Ping to draft the constitution. Everyone knows this. Every time a command is issued, Ping claims the credit, saying "Only I could do this."' The king grew angry and distanced Qu Ping.

sentences.zh: 屈平疾王聽之不聰也，讒諂之蔽明也，邪曲之害公也，方正之不容也，故憂愁幽思而作離騷。

translations.literal: Qu Ping grieved king listening not wise, slander flattery blocked clarity, crooked harmed justice, upright not tolerated, therefore worried sorrowful deep thought composed Li Sao.
translations.idiomatic: Qu Ping was pained that the king's judgment was so easily clouded, that slander and flattery obscured his wisdom, that the crooked and corrupt harmed the public good, and that the upright found no place at court. And so, in deep sorrow and brooding anguish, he composed the 'Encountering Sorrow.'

sentences.zh: 離騷者，猶離憂也。

translations.literal: 'Li Sao' like 'li you' (leaving sorrow).
translations.idiomatic: The title 'Encountering Sorrow' means, in essence, to be beset by grief.

sentences.zh: 夫天者，人之始也；

translations.literal: That heaven, people beginning;
translations.idiomatic: Heaven is the origin of all people;

sentences.zh: 父母者，人之本也。

translations.literal: Parents, people root.
translations.idiomatic: parents are the root of every person.

sentences.zh: 人窮則反本，故勞苦倦極，未嘗不呼天也；

translations.literal: People poor then return to roots, therefore labor toil exhausted extreme, never not call heaven;
translations.idiomatic: When people are driven to extremity, they return to their roots. Thus, in the depths of toil and exhaustion, they never fail to cry out to Heaven;

sentences.zh: 疾痛慘怛，未嘗不呼父母也。

translations.literal: Illness pain bitter sorrow, never not call parents.
translations.idiomatic: in the anguish of sickness and suffering, they never fail to call out to their parents.

sentences.zh: 屈平正道直行，竭忠盡智以事其君，讒人閒之，可謂窮矣。

translations.literal: Qu Ping correct path straight conduct, exhausted loyalty wisdom to serve his lord, slanderers separated them, can be called poor indeed.
translations.idiomatic: Qu Ping walked the straight path and acted with integrity, exhausting his loyalty and wisdom in service to his lord, yet slanderers drove a wedge between them. One may truly say he was driven to the uttermost extremity.

sentences.zh: 信而見疑，忠而被謗，能無怨乎？

translations.literal: Faithful yet seen suspicious, loyal yet slandered, can have no resentment?
translations.idiomatic: To be trusted yet met with suspicion, to be loyal yet slandered — how could he not feel resentment?

sentences.zh: 屈平之作離騷，蓋自怨生也。

translations.literal: Qu Ping composed Li Sao, probably self-resignation born.
translations.idiomatic: Qu Ping's composition of the 'Encountering Sorrow' was born from just such resentment.

sentences.zh: 國風好色而不淫，小雅怨誹而不亂。

translations.literal: Guo Feng loves beauty not licentious, Xiao Ya resents criticizes not chaotic.
translations.idiomatic: The 'Airs of the States' delights in beauty without becoming licentious; the 'Lesser Odes' expresses resentment without descending into disorder.

sentences.zh: 若離騷者，可謂兼之矣。

translations.literal: As for Li Sao, can be said combines them.
translations.idiomatic: The 'Encountering Sorrow' may be said to unite both these virtues.

sentences.zh: 上稱帝嚳，下道齊桓，中述湯武，以刺世事。

translations.literal: Above mentions Emperor Ku, below speaks Duke Huan, middle describes Tang Wu, to criticize current affairs.
translations.idiomatic: It invokes Emperor Ku in the remote past, speaks of Duke Huan of Qi in recent times, and recounts the deeds of Tang and Wu in between — all to cast light on the affairs of the present age.

sentences.zh: 明道德之廣崇，治亂之條貫，靡不畢見。

translations.literal: Clarifies morality broad elevation, governance chaos systematic order, nothing not completely seen.
translations.idiomatic: It illuminates the breadth and grandeur of moral virtue and the patterns underlying order and chaos, leaving nothing unexamined.

sentences.zh: 其文約，其辭微，其志絜，其行廉，其稱文小而其指極大，舉類邇而見義遠。

translations.literal: Its text concise, its words subtle, its will pure, its conduct clean, its references small yet its meaning extremely great, cites nearby examples yet sees far justice.
translations.idiomatic: Its language is concise, its diction subtle, its aspirations pure, its conduct unblemished. Its allusions may seem trifling, but their significance is vast; the examples it draws upon are near at hand, yet the principles they illuminate reach far.

sentences.zh: 其志絜，故其稱物芳。

translations.literal: Its will pure, therefore its named things fragrant.
translations.idiomatic: Because its aspirations are pure, it speaks of fragrant things.

sentences.zh: 其行廉，故死而不容自疏。

translations.literal: Its conduct clean, therefore died not allowing self distant.
translations.idiomatic: Because its conduct is unblemished, he would sooner die than allow himself to be estranged from virtue.

sentences.zh: 濯淖汙泥之中，蟬蛻於濁穢，以浮游塵埃之外，不獲世之滋垢，皭然泥而不滓者也。

translations.literal: Washed mud filth midst, cicada molted foulness, to float roam dust outside, not obtaining world accumulated filth, pure unstained mud not dregs.
translations.idiomatic: He cleansed himself amid the mire and filth, shed his skin like a cicada leaving behind corruption, and floated free beyond the dust of the world. He refused to let the grime of his age stain him — a man who passed through the mud yet emerged without a blemish.

sentences.zh: 推此志也，雖與日月爭光可也。

translations.literal: Pushing this ambition, even with sun moon contend light possible.
translations.idiomatic: Judging by such resolve, one may say that his brilliance could rival even the sun and moon.

translations.literal: Qu Ping resented that the king listened without wisdom, that slanderers blocked clarity, that crookedness harmed justice, that uprightness was not tolerated. Thus he worried and brooded in sorrow and composed 'Li Sao'. 'Li Sao' means departing sorrow. Heaven is the beginning of man; Parents are man's foundation. When man is in distress he returns to fundamentals, thus when exhausted by toil and weariness, one inevitably cries to heaven; When pained by illness and suffering, one inevitably cries to parents. Qu Ping walked the straight path, exhausted loyalty and wisdom serving his lord. Slanderers came between them - he can be called destitute. Trusted yet suspected, loyal yet slandered - how could he not resent? Qu Ping's composition of 'Li Sao' arose from self-pity. 'Guo Feng' delights in beauty without licentiousness, 'Xiao Ya' resents and satirizes without disorder. As for 'Li Sao', it can be said to combine both. Above it praises Emperor Ku, below it describes Duke Huan of Qi, in the middle it narrates Tang and Wu, to criticize contemporary affairs. It illuminates the vast sublimity of morality, the systematic principles of order and chaos - nothing is not fully revealed. Its text is concise, its diction subtle, its ambition pure, its conduct honest. Its language is modest but its meaning extremely grand, citing near examples yet revealing far-reaching principles. Its ambition pure, thus it praises fragrant things. Its conduct honest, thus in death it would not let itself be distant. Washing in filthy mud, shedding like a cicada from impurity, to float and roam beyond the dust, not acquiring the world's defilement, pure white mud yet unstained. Extending this ambition, even contending with sun and moon for brilliance is possible.

sentences.zh: 屈平既絀，其後秦欲伐齊，齊與楚從親，惠王患之，乃令張儀詳去秦，厚幣委質事楚，曰：「秦甚憎齊，齊與楚從親，楚誠能絕齊，秦願獻商、於之地六百里。」

translations.literal: Qu Ping already dismissed, after this Qin wished attack Qi, Qi with Chu allied closely, Hui Wang worried it, then had Zhang Yi pretend leave Qin, thick gifts humble serve Chu, said: 'Qin greatly hates Qi, Qi with Chu allied closely. Chu truly can break Qi, Qin wishes offer Shang Yu lands 600 li.'
translations.idiomatic: After Qu Ping had been dismissed, Qin wished to attack Qi. Since Qi and Chu had formed a close alliance, King Hui of Qin grew alarmed. He dispatched Zhang Yi, who feigned departure from Qin's service and arrived in Chu bearing lavish gifts, pledging himself as a vassal. Zhang Yi told the king of Chu: 'Qin despises Qi, and Qi has allied itself closely with Chu. If Chu would truly sever its ties with Qi, Qin is willing to cede six hundred li of the Shang and Yu territories.'

sentences.zh: 楚懷王貪而信張儀，遂絕齊，使使如秦受地。

translations.literal: Chu Huai Wang greedy believed Zhang Yi, thus broke Qi, sent envoy to Qin receive land.
translations.idiomatic: King Huai of Chu, blinded by greed, believed Zhang Yi. He severed relations with Qi and sent an envoy to Qin to claim the promised territory.

sentences.zh: 張儀詐之曰：「儀與王約六里，不聞六百里。」

translations.literal: Zhang Yi deceived them said: 'Yi with king agreed six li, not heard six hundred li.'
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Yi deceived them, saying: 'I agreed with my king on six li. I never heard anything about six hundred.'

sentences.zh: 楚使怒去，歸告懷王。

translations.literal: Chu envoy angered left, returned told Huai Wang.
translations.idiomatic: The Chu envoy departed in fury and returned to report to King Huai.

sentences.zh: 懷王怒，大興師伐秦。

translations.literal: Huai Wang angered, greatly raised troops attacked Qin.
translations.idiomatic: King Huai was enraged and raised a great army to attack Qin.

sentences.zh: 秦發兵擊之，大破楚師於丹、淅，斬首八萬，虜楚將屈丐，遂取楚之漢中地。

translations.literal: Qin sent troops struck them, greatly defeated Chu army at Dan Xi, beheaded eighty thousand, captured Chu general Qu Gai, thus took Chu Hanzhong land.
translations.idiomatic: Qin sent its forces to meet them and dealt a crushing defeat to the Chu army at Dan and Xi, beheading eighty thousand soldiers and capturing the Chu general Qu Gai. Qin then seized the Hanzhong territories of Chu.

sentences.zh: 懷王乃悉發國中兵以深入擊秦，戰於藍田。

translations.literal: Huai Wang then sent all state troops to deeply attack Qin, battled at Lantian.
translations.idiomatic: King Huai then mobilized every soldier in the realm for a deep thrust into Qin, and the two sides clashed at Lantian.

sentences.zh: 魏聞之，襲楚至鄧。

translations.literal: Wei heard it, attacked Chu to Deng.
translations.idiomatic: Wei, learning of this, launched a surprise attack on Chu and advanced as far as Deng.

sentences.zh: 楚兵懼，自秦歸。

translations.literal: Chu troops feared, from Qin returned.
translations.idiomatic: The Chu forces grew fearful and withdrew from Qin.

sentences.zh: 而齊竟怒不救楚，楚大困。

translations.literal: And Qi finally angered not rescued Chu, Chu greatly distressed.
translations.idiomatic: Qi, still furious at Chu's betrayal, refused to come to its aid, and Chu was left in dire straits.

translations.literal: Qu Ping having been demoted, afterward Qin wanted to attack Qi. Qi and Chu had allied closely. Qin King Hui worried, thus ordered Zhang Yi to pretend to leave Qin, offer rich gifts and serve Chu, saying: 'Qin greatly hates Qi. Qi and Chu have allied closely. If Chu can truly break with Qi, Qin wishes to offer the lands of Shang and Yu, six hundred li.' Chu King Huai, greedy, believed Zhang Yi, thus broke with Qi and sent envoys to Qin to receive land. Zhang Yi deceived saying: 'Yi and the king agreed to six li, not six hundred li.' The Chu envoy angrily left, returned and reported to King Huai. King Huai angered, greatly raised troops to attack Qin. Qin sent troops to strike them, greatly defeated Chu army at Dan and Xi, beheaded eighty thousand, captured Chu general Qu Gai, thus took Chu's Han River region. King Huai then mobilized all troops in the state for deep invasion to attack Qin, battled at Lan Tian. Wei heard of it, raided Chu to Deng. Chu troops feared, returned from Qin. Yet Qi ultimately angered and did not rescue Chu, Chu greatly distressed.

sentences.zh: 明年，秦割漢中地與楚以和。

translations.literal: Next year, Qin ceded Hanzhong land to Chu for peace.
translations.idiomatic: The following year, Qin offered to cede the Hanzhong territory to Chu in exchange for peace.

sentences.zh: 楚王曰：「不願得地，願得張儀而甘心焉。」

translations.literal: Chu Wang said: 'Not wish get land, wish get Zhang Yi satisfy hatred.'
translations.idiomatic: The king of Chu declared: 'I do not want the land. I want Zhang Yi — only then will my heart be satisfied.'

sentences.zh: 張儀聞，乃曰：「以一儀而當漢中地，臣請往如楚。」

translations.literal: Zhang Yi heard, then said: 'With one Zhang Yi match Hanzhong land, minister requests go to Chu.'
translations.idiomatic: When Zhang Yi heard this, he said: 'If one Zhang Yi is worth the Hanzhong territory, then let me go to Chu.'

sentences.zh: 如楚，又因厚幣用事者臣靳尚，而設詭辯於懷王之寵姬鄭袖。

translations.literal: Went to Chu, moreover used thick gifts influential minister Jin Shang, set up deceptive arguments with Huai Wang favored concubine Zheng Xiu.
translations.idiomatic: He went to Chu and bribed the powerful minister Jin Shang with lavish gifts, then contrived through cunning arguments to win over King Huai's favored consort, Zheng Xiu.

sentences.zh: 懷王竟聽鄭袖，復釋去張儀。

translations.literal: Huai Wang finally listened Zheng Xiu, again released Zhang Yi.
translations.idiomatic: In the end, King Huai heeded Zheng Xiu and released Zhang Yi once again.

sentences.zh: 是時屈平既疏，不復在位，使於齊，顧反，諫懷王曰：「何不殺張儀？」

translations.literal: This time Qu Ping already alienated, no longer in position, sent to Qi, looked back, advised Huai Wang said: 'Why not kill Zhang Yi?'
translations.idiomatic: At that time, Qu Ping had already been estranged and was no longer in office. He had been sent on a mission to Qi, and upon his return, he remonstrated with King Huai: 'Why did you not execute Zhang Yi?'

sentences.zh: 懷王悔，追張儀不及。

translations.literal: Huai Wang regretted, pursued Zhang Yi could not catch.
translations.idiomatic: King Huai was filled with regret and sent men to pursue Zhang Yi, but they could not catch him.

translations.literal: Next year, Qin ceded Han River region to Chu for peace. Chu king said: 'I do not wish land, I wish Zhang Yi to satisfy my hatred.' Zhang Yi heard, thus said: 'Using one Yi to exchange for Han River region, minister requests to go to Chu.' Went to Chu, again used rich gifts to make the influential minister Jin Shang serve him, and set deceptive arguments through King Huai's favored concubine Zheng Xiu. King Huai ultimately listened to Zheng Xiu, again released Zhang Yi. At this time Qu Ping was already estranged, no longer in position. Sent to Qi, on return advised King Huai: 'Why not kill Zhang Yi?' King Huai regretted, pursued Zhang Yi but could not catch him.

sentences.zh: 其後諸侯共擊楚，大破之，殺其將唐眛。

translations.literal: After this feudal lords together attacked Chu, greatly defeated them, killed their general Tang Mei.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward, the feudal lords joined forces against Chu, dealt it a crushing defeat, and slew its general Tang Mei.

translations.literal: Afterward the feudal lords together attacked Chu, greatly defeated them, killed their general Tang Mei.

sentences.zh: 時秦昭王與楚婚，欲與懷王會。

translations.literal: At this time Qin Zhao Wang married with Chu, wished to meet with Huai Wang.
translations.idiomatic: At that time, King Zhao of Qin had formed a marriage alliance with Chu and wished to meet with King Huai.

sentences.zh: 懷王欲行，屈平曰：「秦虎狼之國，不可信，不如毋行。」

translations.literal: Huai Wang wished go, Qu Ping said: 'Qin tiger wolf country, cannot trust, better not go.'
translations.idiomatic: King Huai intended to go, but Qu Ping warned him: 'Qin is a land of tigers and wolves. It cannot be trusted. Better not to go.'

sentences.zh: 懷王稚子子蘭勸王行：「柰何絕秦歡！」

translations.literal: Huai Wang young son Zi Lan advised king go: 'How break Qin friendship!'
translations.idiomatic: But King Huai's youngest son, Zi Lan, urged the king to go: 'How can we sever our friendship with Qin!'

sentences.zh: 懷王卒行。

translations.literal: Huai Wang finally went.
translations.idiomatic: In the end, King Huai went.

sentences.zh: 入武關，秦伏兵絕其後，因留懷王，以求割地。

translations.literal: Entered Wuguan, Qin ambush troops cut off their rear, thus detained Huai Wang to demand ceding land.
translations.idiomatic: When he entered the Wu Pass, Qin troops sprang from ambush and cut off his retreat. They detained King Huai and demanded that he cede territory.

sentences.zh: 懷王怒，不聽。

translations.literal: Huai Wang angered, did not listen.
translations.idiomatic: King Huai was furious and refused.

sentences.zh: 亡走趙，趙不內。

translations.literal: Fled to Zhao, Zhao not admit.
translations.idiomatic: He escaped and fled to Zhao, but Zhao refused him entry.

sentences.zh: 復之秦，竟死於秦而歸葬。

translations.literal: Returned to Qin, finally died in Qin returned for burial.
translations.idiomatic: He was returned to Qin, where he eventually died. His body was sent home for burial.

translations.literal: At this time Qin King Zhao married into Chu, wished to meet with King Huai. King Huai wished to go. Qu Ping said: 'Qin is a tiger wolf state, cannot be trusted, better not to go.' King Huai's young son Zi Lan urged the king to go: 'How can we cut off Qin's goodwill!' King Huai ultimately went. Entered Wu Pass, Qin ambushed troops cut off his rear, thus detained King Huai to demand territory cession. King Huai angered, would not listen. Fled to Zhao, Zhao would not admit him. Returned to Qin, ultimately died in Qin and was returned for burial.

sentences.zh: 長子頃襄王立，以其弟子蘭為令尹。

translations.literal: Eldest son King Qingxiang established, made his younger brother Zi Lan prime minister.
translations.idiomatic: The eldest son ascended the throne as King Qingxiang and appointed his younger brother Zi Lan as Prime Minister.

sentences.zh: 楚人既咎子蘭以勸懷王入秦而不反也。

translations.literal: Chu people already blamed Zi Lan for advising Huai Wang enter Qin not return.
translations.idiomatic: The people of Chu already blamed Zi Lan for having urged King Huai to enter Qin, whence he never returned.

translations.literal: Eldest son King Qingxiang ascended, made his younger brother Zi Lan prime minister. Chu people already blamed Zi Lan for urging King Huai to enter Qin without return.

sentences.zh: 屈平既嫉之，雖放流，睠顧楚國，系心懷王，不忘欲反，冀幸君之一悟，俗之一改也。

translations.literal: Qu Ping already hated him, though banished, fondly regarded Chu state, tied heart Huai Wang, not forget wished return, hoped king one awakening, customs one change.
translations.idiomatic: Qu Ping loathed Zi Lan. Though banished, he remained devoted to the state of Chu. His heart was bound to King Huai, and he never ceased longing for his return to court, hoping that the king might one day awaken to the truth and that the corruption of the age might be reformed.

sentences.zh: 其存君興國而欲反覆之，一篇之中三致志焉。

translations.literal: His preserve lord revive country wish reverse it, one chapter three times expressed will.
translations.idiomatic: His desire to preserve his lord and restore the state, to reverse its decline — this resolve is expressed three times within a single poem.

sentences.zh: 然終無可柰何，故不可以反，卒以此見懷王之終不悟也。

translations.literal: Yet finally nothing could be done, therefore could not return, finally thus saw Huai Wang final lack of awakening.
translations.idiomatic: Yet in the end, nothing could be done. He could not return to court, and so he came to see at last that King Huai would never awaken.

sentences.zh: 人君無愚智賢不肖，莫不欲求忠以自為，舉賢以自佐，然亡國破家相隨屬，而聖君治國累世而不見者，其所謂忠者不忠，而所謂賢者不賢也。

translations.literal: Rulers regardless foolish wise worthy unworthy, none not wish seek loyal for themselves, raise worthy assist themselves, yet ruined states broken families follow succession, yet sage kings govern states generations not appear - what they called loyal not loyal, what they called worthy not worthy.
translations.idiomatic: Every ruler, whether foolish or wise, worthy or unworthy, wishes to find loyal ministers to serve him and to elevate the worthy to assist him. Yet states collapse and families are ruined in unbroken succession, while sage rulers who bring lasting order are not seen for generations on end. This is because those they called loyal were not truly loyal, and those they called worthy were not truly worthy.

sentences.zh: 懷王以不知忠臣之分，故內惑於鄭袖，外欺於張儀，疏屈平而信上官大夫、令尹子蘭。

translations.literal: Huai Wang because not know loyal ministers distinction, therefore internally confused by Zheng Xiu, externally deceived by Zhang Yi, alienated Qu Ping trusted Chamberlain Shangguan, Prime Minister Zi Lan.
translations.idiomatic: Because King Huai could not distinguish a loyal minister from a false one, he was beguiled within by Zheng Xiu and deceived without by Zhang Yi. He cast aside Qu Ping and placed his trust in Grand Master Shangguan and the Prime Minister Zi Lan.

sentences.zh: 兵挫地削，亡其六郡，身客死於秦，為天下笑。

translations.literal: Army defeated land reduced, lost six commanderies, body died as guest in Qin, became world laughingstock.
translations.idiomatic: His armies were routed, his territory stripped away, and six commanderies were lost. He himself died a captive in Qin, and became a laughingstock to all under Heaven.

sentences.zh: 此不知人之禍也。

translations.literal: This not know people calamity.
translations.idiomatic: Such is the calamity of failing to know men.

sentences.zh: 《易》曰：「井泄不食，為我心惻，可以汲。

translations.literal: 'Yi Jing' said: 'Well leaks not drunk, makes my heart sorrowful, can draw water.
translations.idiomatic: The Book of Changes says: 'The well seeps but none drink from it — this grieves my heart. Yet water may still be drawn.

sentences.zh: 王明，并受其福。」

translations.literal: King enlightened, all receive its blessing.'
translations.idiomatic: When the king is wise, all share in his blessings.'

sentences.zh: 王之不明，豈足福哉！

translations.literal: King lack of enlightenment, how sufficient blessing!
translations.idiomatic: But when the king lacks wisdom, how can there be any blessing at all!

translations.literal: Qu Ping having envied this, though banished, gazed back at Chu state, heart attached to King Huai, not forgetting desire to return, hoping the lord would one day awaken, customs would one day change. His preserving the lord and reviving the state while wishing to overturn them - in one poem three times expressing this ambition. Yet ultimately nothing could be done, thus could not return, finally showed King Huai's final lack of awakening. Rulers, whether foolish or wise, worthy or unworthy, all wish to seek loyalty for themselves, promote worthies to assist themselves. Yet ruined states and broken families follow one after another, while sage lords govern states for generations without being seen. What they call loyal is not loyal, what they call worthy is not worthy. King Huai because he did not know the distinction of loyal ministers, thus internally deluded by Zheng Xiu, externally deceived by Zhang Yi, distanced Qu Ping while trusting Grand Chamberlain and Prime Minister Zi Lan. Troops defeated territory reduced, lost six commanderies, person died as guest in Qin, became world's laughter. This is the calamity of not knowing people. 'Yi' says: 'Well leaks not drunk, makes my heart sorrowful, can draw water. King enlightened, all receive its blessing.' The king's lack of enlightenment, how can it bring blessing!

sentences.zh: 令尹子蘭聞之大怒，卒使上官大夫短屈原於頃襄王，頃襄王怒而遷之。

translations.literal: Prime Minister Zi Lan heard greatly angered, finally made Chamberlain Shangguan slander Qu Yuan to King Qingxiang, King Qingxiang angered banished him.
translations.idiomatic: When Prime Minister Zi Lan heard of this, he was enraged. He had Grand Master Shangguan speak against Qu Yuan before King Qingxiang. The king, in his anger, banished Qu Yuan.

translations.literal: Prime Minister Zi Lan heard of it greatly angered, ultimately had Grand Chamberlain slander Qu Yuan to King Qingxiang. King Qingxiang angered and banished him.

sentences.zh: 屈原至於江濱，被髪行吟澤畔。

translations.literal: Qu Yuan arrived at Jiang shore, disheveled hair walking reciting marsh side.
translations.idiomatic: Qu Yuan came to the banks of the river. With his hair unbound, he wandered along the marshy shore, chanting as he walked.

sentences.zh: 顏色憔悴，形容枯槁。

translations.literal: Color haggard, appearance withered.
translations.idiomatic: His face was haggard, his body gaunt and wasted.

sentences.zh: 漁父見而問之曰：「子非三閭大夫歟？

translations.literal: Fisherman saw asked him said: 'You not San Lu official?
translations.idiomatic: A fisherman saw him and asked: 'Are you not the Grand Master of the Three Wards?

sentences.zh: 何故而至此？」

translations.literal: Why come to this?'
translations.idiomatic: What has brought you to this?'

sentences.zh: 屈原曰：「舉世混濁而我獨清，眾人皆醉而我獨醒，是以見放。」

translations.literal: Qu Yuan said: 'Whole world turbid yet I alone clear, everyone drunk yet I alone sober, therefore banished.'
translations.idiomatic: Qu Yuan replied: 'The whole world is muddied, yet I alone remain clear. All men are drunk, yet I alone am sober. That is why I have been cast out.'

sentences.zh: 漁父曰：「夫聖人者，不凝滯於物而能與世推移。

translations.literal: Fisherman said: 'Sage people not congealed stagnant in things can shift with world.
translations.idiomatic: The fisherman said: 'A sage does not cling rigidly to things, but moves with the current of the world.

sentences.zh: 舉世混濁，何不隨其流而揚其波？

translations.literal: Whole world turbid, why not follow its flow raise its waves?
translations.idiomatic: If the whole world is muddied, why not go with its flow and ride its waves?

sentences.zh: 眾人皆醉，何不餔其糟而啜其醨？

translations.literal: Everyone drunk, why not eat its dregs drink its thin wine?
translations.idiomatic: If all men are drunk, why not eat the dregs and sip the thin wine along with them?

sentences.zh: 何故懷瑾握瑜而自令見放為？」

translations.literal: Why hold jade embrace purity yet self make see banished?'
translations.idiomatic: Why cling so tightly to your jade and jewels that you bring banishment upon yourself?'

sentences.zh: 屈原曰：「吾聞之，新沐者必彈冠，新浴者必振衣，人又誰能以身之察察，受物之汶汶者乎！

translations.literal: Qu Yuan said: 'I heard, newly bathed must flick cap, newly washed must shake clothes, who can with body clean, receive things dirty!
translations.idiomatic: Qu Yuan replied: 'I have heard that one who has just washed his hair must dust off his cap, and one who has just bathed must shake out his robes. How can anyone, with a body so clean, allow himself to be sullied by the filth of the world!

sentences.zh: 寧赴常流而葬乎江魚腹中耳，又安能以皓皓之白而蒙世俗之溫蠖乎！」

translations.literal: Rather go constant flow bury Jiang fish belly, how can with bright white covered world dust!'
translations.idiomatic: I would sooner cast myself into the river's ceaseless flow and be buried in the bellies of the fish, than let my radiant purity be smothered by the dust of this vulgar world!'

translations.literal: Qu Yuan reached the Jiang River shore, disheveled wandering reciting by the marsh. Complexion haggard, form withered. Fisherman saw and asked him: 'Are you not the San Lu official? Why have you come to this?' Qu Yuan said: 'The whole world is muddy yet I alone am clear, everyone is drunk yet I alone am sober, thus I am banished.' Fisherman said: 'Sages do not congeal in things and can shift with the age. Whole world muddy, why not follow the current and raise its waves? Everyone drunk, why not drink the dregs and sip the thin wine? Why embrace jade and hold pearl yet make yourself banished?' Qu Yuan said: 'I have heard, newly washed must dust the cap, newly bathed must shake the clothes. Moreover who can with body's purity receive things' defilement? Rather enter the constant current and be buried in the Jiang fish belly, how can I with gleaming white submit to the world's dust and filth!'

sentences.zh: 乃作懷沙之賦。

translations.literal: Then composed Huai Sha assignment.
translations.idiomatic: He then composed the rhapsody 'Embracing Sand.'

sentences.zh: 其辭曰：

translations.literal: Its words said:
translations.idiomatic: Its words read:

translations.literal: Thus composed 'Huai Sha' rhapsody. Its words say:

sentences.zh: 陶陶孟夏兮，草木莽莽。

translations.literal: Bustling mid-summer, vegetation lush.
translations.idiomatic: In the warmth of early summer, the grasses and trees grow wild and lush.

sentences.zh: 傷懷永哀兮，汩徂南土。

translations.literal: Wounded heart eternal sorrow, rushing to southern land.
translations.idiomatic: My wounded heart aches with endless sorrow, as I am swept away to the southern lands.

sentences.zh: 眴兮窈窈，孔靜幽墨。

translations.literal: Dazzling deep dark, greatly quiet mysterious dark.
translations.idiomatic: All is dim and fathomless, a vast silence shrouded in darkness.

sentences.zh: 冤結紆軫兮，離愍之長鞠；

translations.literal: Grievance knotted twisted pained, separated pity long drawn out;
translations.idiomatic: My grievances coil and knot within me, and the anguish of my exile stretches on without end;

sentences.zh: 撫情效志兮，俛詘以自抑。

translations.literal: Caress feelings follow will, bow bend self restrain.
translations.idiomatic: I steady my feelings and hold fast to my purpose, bowing low to restrain myself.

translations.literal: Bright fifth month, plants and trees dense. Wounded heart eternal sorrow, hastening to southern lands. Gazing deep dark, extremely quiet secluded dark. Grievance knotted twisting pained, sorrow's long suffering; Caressing feelings examining ambition, bending to restrain oneself.

sentences.zh: 刓方以為圜兮，常度未替；

translations.literal: Blunt square make round, constant measure not changed;
translations.idiomatic: They would grind what is square to make it round, yet the eternal standard remains unchanged;

sentences.zh: 易初本由兮，君子所鄙。

translations.literal: Change beginning follow by, gentleman despised.
translations.idiomatic: to abandon one's original nature and principles is what the true gentleman despises.

sentences.zh: 章畫職墨兮，前度未改；

translations.literal: Clear markings duty ink, previous measure not changed;
translations.idiomatic: The compass lines are drawn and the ink marks set; the old standards have not been altered;

sentences.zh: 內直質重兮，大人所盛。

translations.literal: Inner straight quality heavy, great man esteemed.
translations.idiomatic: inner rectitude and steadfast substance are what the great man esteems.

sentences.zh: 巧匠不斲兮，孰察其揆正？

translations.literal: Skillful craftsman not chisel, who discerns its standard correctness?
translations.idiomatic: If the skillful craftsman does not carve, who will discern that the timber is true?

sentences.zh: 玄文幽處兮，矇謂之不章；

translations.literal: Dark writings hidden place, blind call not clear;
translations.idiomatic: Profound patterns lie hidden in the deep, and the blind call them obscure;

sentences.zh: 離婁微睇兮，瞽以為無明。

translations.literal: Li Lou slight glance, blind consider no sight.
translations.idiomatic: Li Lou barely glances, and the sightless declare there is no light.

sentences.zh: 變白而為黑兮，倒上以為下。

translations.literal: Change white become black, invert upper become lower.
translations.idiomatic: They turn white to black and reverse what is above to make it below.

sentences.zh: 鳳皇在笯兮，雞雉翔舞。

translations.literal: Phoenix in cage, chickens pheasants fly dance.
translations.idiomatic: The phoenix is caged, while the chickens and pheasants soar and dance.

sentences.zh: 同糅玉石兮，一而相量。

translations.literal: Mix jade stone together, one yet measure each other.
translations.idiomatic: Jade and common stone are jumbled together and weighed as one.

sentences.zh: 夫黨人之鄙妒兮，羌不知吾所臧。

translations.literal: That faction people base jealousy, utterly not know my treasures.
translations.idiomatic: Those petty factions in their base jealousy have no inkling of the worth I hold within.

translations.literal: Rounding square to make round, constant measure unchanged; Changing beginning following precedent, gentleman despises. Pattern drawing using ink, previous measure unchanged; Inner straight substantial heavy, great man values. Skilled artisan not hews, who examines its standard correctness? Dark patterns hidden place, blind calls it not bright; Li Lou subtle gaze, blind considers it no sight. Changing white to black, inverting top to bottom. Phoenix in cage, chickens fly dance. Mixing jade stone together, same yet compared. Faction men's base jealousy, utterly unknown my treasure.

sentences.zh: 任重載盛兮，陷滯而不濟；

translations.literal: Bear heavy load full load, trapped stuck not cross;
translations.idiomatic: The burden I bear is heavy, the load beyond measure, and I am mired and cannot cross;

sentences.zh: 懷瑾握瑜兮，窮不得余所示。

translations.literal: Hold jade embrace purity, poor not get I show.
translations.idiomatic: I clasp my jade and hold my jewels, but in my extremity, I find no one to whom I may show them.

sentences.zh: 邑犬群吠兮，吠所怪也；

translations.literal: Village dogs pack bark, bark what strange;
translations.idiomatic: The village dogs bark in a pack — they bark at whatever seems strange to them;

sentences.zh: 誹駿疑桀兮，固庸態也。

translations.literal: Slander excellent suspect Jie, originally common attitude.
translations.idiomatic: to slander the excellent and suspect the outstanding is simply the way of the mediocre.

sentences.zh: 文質疏內兮，眾不知吾之異采；

translations.literal: Culture substance coarse inner, crowd not know my different colors;
translations.idiomatic: My refinement and substance lie within, unadorned — the crowd does not see my singular brilliance;

sentences.zh: 材樸委積兮，莫知余之所有。

translations.literal: Timber simple piled up, none know I have.
translations.idiomatic: my timber is rough-hewn and heaped aside, and none know what I possess.

sentences.zh: 重仁襲義兮，謹厚以為豐；

translations.literal: Heavy benevolence layer righteousness, careful thick as abundance;
translations.idiomatic: I pile benevolence upon righteousness, scrupulous and generous in abundance;

sentences.zh: 重華不可牾兮，孰知余之從容！

translations.literal: Chong Hua cannot oppose, who knows my composed!
translations.idiomatic: Even Emperor Shun could not fault me — yet who knows the composure in my heart!

sentences.zh: 迸固有不并兮，豈知其故也？

translations.literal: Burst solid have not together, how know its reason?
translations.idiomatic: That the worthy and the base should not stand together has ever been so — yet who grasps the reason why?

sentences.zh: 湯禹久遠兮，邈不可慕也。

translations.literal: Tang Yu distant, remote cannot admire.
translations.idiomatic: Tang and Yu are far away in the distant past, too remote to serve as models.

sentences.zh: 懲違改忿兮，抑心而自彊；

translations.literal: Punish violation change anger, repress heart self strengthen;
translations.idiomatic: I correct my faults and master my anger, restraining my heart to strengthen my resolve;

sentences.zh: 離湣而不遷兮，願志之有象。

translations.literal: Separated pity not move, wish ambition have image.
translations.idiomatic: though beset by sorrow, I will not waver — I wish only that my purpose may find form.

sentences.zh: 進路北次兮，日昧昧其將暮；

translations.literal: Advance road north lodge, day dim its will dusk;
translations.idiomatic: I journey northward and make camp, but the day grows dim as dusk approaches;

sentences.zh: 含憂虞哀兮，限之以大故。

translations.literal: Contain worry fear sorrow, limited by great matter.
translations.idiomatic: sorrow and dread fill my breast, and I am bound by the finality of death.

translations.literal: Bearing heavy carrying full, sinking stuck not crossing; Embracing jade holding pearl, destitute cannot show to others. City dogs pack barking, barking what they consider strange; Slandering steeds doubting Jie, originally common attitude. Literary quality sparse inner, crowd unknown my different colors; Talents unhewn accumulated, none know my possessions. Repeated benevolence repeated righteousness, careful substantial as abundance; Repeated Hua cannot oppose, who knows my ease! Banished firmly not together, how know its reason? Tang Yu long distant, far cannot admire. Punished violation changed anger, suppressed heart self-strengthened; Departed sorrow not moved, wished ambition have model. Advanced road north lodged, day dim dim will evening; Contained worry feared sorrow, limited by great cause.

sentences.zh: 亂曰：浩浩沅、湘兮，分流汨兮。

translations.literal: Chaos says: Vast Yuan Xiang, divide flow Mi.
translations.idiomatic: The envoi says: Vast are the Yuan and the Xiang, their waters parting as they flow into the Mi.

sentences.zh: 修路幽拂兮，道遠忽兮。

translations.literal: Long road dark brush, way far suddenly.
translations.idiomatic: The long road stretches dim and tangled; the way ahead disappears into the distance.

sentences.zh: 曾唫恒悲兮，永嘆慨兮。

translations.literal: Increasing sigh constant sorrow, eternal sigh indignant.
translations.idiomatic: My sighs deepen and my sorrow never lifts; an unending lamentation wells within me.

sentences.zh: 世既莫吾知兮，人心不可謂兮。

translations.literal: World already none know me, human heart cannot predict.
translations.idiomatic: The world no longer knows me, and the human heart cannot be fathomed.

sentences.zh: 懷情抱質兮，獨無匹兮。

translations.literal: Embrace feelings hold quality, alone without match.
translations.idiomatic: I hold my feelings close and cling to my integrity — alone, without a peer.

sentences.zh: 伯樂既歿兮，驥將焉程兮？

translations.literal: Bo Le already dead, steed where appraise?
translations.idiomatic: Now that Bo Le is dead, who will judge the worth of a fine steed?

sentences.zh: 人生稟命兮，各有所錯兮。

translations.literal: Human life receive fate, each has placement.
translations.idiomatic: In this life, each person receives a fate, and each has a place to which he is assigned.

sentences.zh: 定心廣志，餘何畏懼兮？

translations.literal: Settle heart broaden ambition, I what fear?
translations.idiomatic: I steady my heart and broaden my resolve — what have I left to fear?

sentences.zh: 曾傷爰哀，永嘆喟兮。

translations.literal: Increasingly wounded beloved sorrow, eternal sigh indignant.
translations.idiomatic: The grief deepens, the sorrow mounts — I sigh and sigh without end.

sentences.zh: 世溷不吾知，心不可謂兮。

translations.literal: World turbid not know me, heart cannot predict.
translations.idiomatic: The world is muddied and does not know me; the heart cannot be spoken of.

sentences.zh: 知死不可讓兮，願勿愛兮。

translations.literal: Know death cannot yield, wish not love.
translations.idiomatic: I know that death cannot be refused — let me not cling to life.

sentences.zh: 明以告君子兮，吾將以為類兮。

translations.literal: Clearly tell gentlemen, I will take as example.
translations.idiomatic: Let me declare this plainly to the gentlemen of the world: I shall make my death a testament.

translations.literal: Chaos says: Vast Yuan Xiang, divided flow Mi. Long road secluded brushed, way far sudden. Layer sighed constant sorrow, eternal sighed grieved. Age already none know me, human heart cannot speak. Embrace feelings hold quality, alone without match. Bo Le already dead, steed where appraise? Human life receives fate, each has placement. Fixed heart broad ambition, I what fear? Layer injured loved sorrow, eternal sighed grieved. Age muddy not know me, heart cannot speak. Know death cannot yield, wish not cherish. Clearly tell gentleman, I will take as example.

sentences.zh: 於是懷石遂自（投）［沈］汨羅以死。

translations.literal: Then embrace stone then self drown Miluo to die.
translations.idiomatic: And so, clasping a stone to his chest, he cast himself into the Miluo River and drowned.

translations.literal: Thus embracing stone self-drowned Mi Luo to die.

sentences.zh: 屈原既死之後，楚有宋玉、唐勒、景差之徒者，皆好辭而以賦見稱；

translations.literal: Qu Yuan already died after, Chu had Song Yu, Tang Le, Jing Chai and others, all good rhetoric and with rhapsodies seen praised;
translations.idiomatic: After Qu Yuan's death, Chu produced such men as Song Yu, Tang Le, and Jing Chai, all of whom were skilled in literary composition and won renown for their rhapsodies;

sentences.zh: 然皆祖屈原之從容辭令，終莫敢直諫。

translations.literal: Yet all followed Qu Yuan composed rhetoric, finally none dared direct admonish.
translations.idiomatic: yet all of them merely imitated Qu Yuan's elegant style, and none ever dared to speak in frank remonstrance.

sentences.zh: 其後楚日以削，數十年竟為秦所滅。

translations.literal: After this Chu day by day diminished, several decades finally by Qin destroyed.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward, Chu declined day by day, and within a few decades it was destroyed by Qin.

translations.literal: Qu Yuan having died after, Chu had Song Yu, Tang Le, Jing Chai and others, all good at rhetoric and famous for rhapsodies; Yet all followed Qu Yuan's ease and rhetoric, ultimately dared not directly remonstrate. Afterward Chu daily diminished, several decades ultimately destroyed by Qin.

sentences.zh: 自屈原沈汨羅後百有餘年，漢有賈生，為長沙王太傅，過湘水，投書以弔屈原。

translations.literal: From Qu Yuan drowned Miluo after hundred plus years, Han had Jia Sheng, was Changsha King Taifu, crossed Xiang water, cast letter to lament Qu Yuan.
translations.idiomatic: More than a hundred years after Qu Yuan drowned in the Miluo, there was a man of the Han named Jia Sheng, who served as Grand Tutor to the King of Changsha. As he crossed the Xiang River, he cast a written elegy into the waters to mourn Qu Yuan.

translations.literal: From Qu Yuan drowning Mi Luo after hundred plus years, Han had Jia Sheng, as Changsha King tutor, passed Xiang water, cast letter to mourn Qu Yuan.

sentences.zh: 賈生名誼，雒陽人也。

translations.literal: Jia Sheng named Yi, Luoyang person.
translations.idiomatic: Jia Sheng, whose given name was Yi, was a native of Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 年十八，以能誦詩屬書聞於郡中。

translations.literal: Age eighteen, able recite poetry compose writings famous in commandery.
translations.idiomatic: By the age of eighteen, he was renowned throughout his commandery for his ability to recite the Odes and compose fine prose.

sentences.zh: 吳廷尉為河南守，聞其秀才，召置門下，甚幸愛。

translations.literal: Wu the Commandant of Justice as Henan governor, heard his talent, summoned placed under his patronage, greatly favored and cherished.
translations.idiomatic: Wu, the Commandant of Justice, was serving as Governor of Henan. When he heard of Jia Yi's talent, he summoned him into his household and treated him with great favor and affection.

sentences.zh: 孝文皇帝初立，聞河南守吳公治平為天下第一，故與李斯同邑而常學事焉，乃徵為廷尉。

translations.literal: Emperor Wen of Han first ascended throne, heard Henan governor Wu Gong governed peacefully ranked first under heaven, formerly from same town as Li Si and often studied under him, then summoned as Commandant of Justice.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Wen first ascended the throne, he heard that Wu Gong, the Governor of Henan, governed so well that he was reckoned the finest administrator in the realm. Wu Gong had come from the same town as Li Si and had often studied under him. The emperor summoned Wu Gong to serve as Commandant of Justice.

sentences.zh: 廷尉乃言賈生年少，頗通諸子百家之書。

translations.literal: The Commandant of Justice then spoke of Jia Sheng, young, quite versed in the writings of the various philosophers and the Hundred Schools.
translations.idiomatic: The Commandant of Justice then spoke of Jia Sheng, saying that though he was young, he was deeply versed in the writings of the various philosophers and the Hundred Schools of thought.

sentences.zh: 文帝召以為博士。

translations.literal: Wen Emperor summoned as erudite.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wen summoned him and appointed him as an Erudite.

translations.literal: Jia Sheng named Yi, Luoyang person. Age eighteen, with ability to recite poetry and books famous in commandery. Wu Court Scribe as Henan governor, heard his outstanding talent, summoned to place under gate, greatly favored loved. Filial Wen emperor initially ascended, heard Henan governor Wu Gong governed peacefully as world first, thus with Li Si same district and often studied affairs from him, thus summoned as Court Scribe. Court Scribe then spoke Jia Sheng young, quite versed in hundred schools books. Wen emperor summoned as erudite.

sentences.zh: 是時賈生年二十餘，最為少。

translations.literal: At this time Jia Sheng age twenty plus, most young.
translations.idiomatic: At that time, Jia Sheng was just over twenty years old — the youngest among them.

sentences.zh: 每詔令議下，諸老先生不能言，賈生盡為之對，人人各如其意所欲出。

translations.literal: Every time edicts discussions sent down, various old masters could not speak, Jia Sheng completely answered for them, everyone each as their intention wished out.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever an edict was sent down for deliberation, the elder scholars could find nothing to say, but Jia Sheng answered every point on their behalf, expressing what each of them had wished to say but could not articulate.

sentences.zh: 諸生於是乃以為能，不及也。

translations.literal: Various scholars then considered him able, not inferior.
translations.idiomatic: The other scholars all acknowledged his ability and conceded that they could not match him.

sentences.zh: 孝文帝說之，超遷，一歲中至太中大夫。

translations.literal: Filial Wen Emperor pleased, promoted extraordinarily, one year to Grand Palace Grandee.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wen was delighted with him and promoted him rapidly. Within a single year, he rose to the rank of Grand Palace Grandee.

translations.literal: At this time Jia Sheng age twenty plus, was youngest. Every time edicts and commands issued for discussion, various old masters could not speak, Jia Sheng completely responded to them, each person as their intent desired. Various scholars then considered him able, could not match. Filial Wen emperor pleased, promoted extraordinarily, one year to Grand Palace Grandee.

sentences.zh: 賈生以為漢興至孝文二十餘年，天下和洽，而固當改正朔，易服色，法制度，定官名，興禮樂，乃悉草具其事儀法，色尚黃，數用五，為官名，悉更秦之法。

translations.literal: Jia Sheng thought Han rise to Filial Wen twenty plus years, world peaceful harmonious, should change calendar, change colors, establish laws systems, fix official names, promote rites music, then completely drafted all matters ceremonies laws, color esteem yellow, number use five, for official names, completely changed Qin laws.
translations.idiomatic: Jia Sheng believed that more than twenty years had passed since the founding of the Han, and that the realm was now at peace. It was therefore time to reform the calendar, change the ritual colors, revise the laws and institutions, establish proper official titles, and promote the rites and music. He drafted a comprehensive plan for all of these measures, proposing yellow as the dynastic color, the number five as the governing numeral, and new official titles — entirely replacing the old Qin system.

sentences.zh: 孝文帝初即位，謙讓未遑也。

translations.literal: Filial Wen Emperor initially ascended, modest yielding not leisure.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wen had only recently ascended the throne and, out of modesty and restraint, had not yet found the occasion to act.

sentences.zh: 諸律令所更定，及列侯悉就國，其說皆自賈生發之。

translations.literal: Various statutes orders what changed fixed, and marquises all go to states, these theories all from Jia Sheng originated.
translations.idiomatic: The various reforms to the statutes and ordinances, as well as the policy of sending all the marquises to their fiefdoms, all originated from Jia Sheng's proposals.

sentences.zh: 於是天子議以為賈生任公卿之位。

translations.literal: Then emperor discussed make Jia Sheng assume duke ministers position.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor then considered promoting Jia Sheng to the rank of one of the great ministers of state.

sentences.zh: 絳、灌、東陽侯、馮敬之屬盡害之，乃短賈生曰：「雒陽之人，年少初學，專欲擅權，紛亂諸事。」

translations.literal: Jiang, Guan, Dongyang Hou, Feng Jing and others all harmed him, then slandered Jia Sheng said: 'Luoyang person, young just studied, monopolize want seize power, confuse various matters.'
translations.idiomatic: But the Marquis of Jiang, Guan Ying, the Marquis of Dongyang, Feng Jing, and others all opposed him. They slandered Jia Sheng, saying: 'This man from Luoyang is young and has barely begun his studies, yet he seeks to monopolize power and throw everything into confusion.'

sentences.zh: 於是天子後亦疏之，不用其議，乃以賈生為長沙王太傅。

translations.literal: Then emperor afterward also distanced him, not used his proposals, then made Jia Sheng Changsha King Taifu.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor afterward also distanced himself from Jia Sheng and ceased to follow his counsel. He appointed Jia Sheng as Grand Tutor to the King of Changsha.

translations.literal: Jia Sheng considered Han rise to Filial Wen twenty plus years, world peaceful harmonious, yet should correct calendar, change clothing colors, establish laws systems, determine official names, promote rites music. Thus completely drafted all affair ceremonies laws, color honored yellow, number used five, for official names, completely changed Qin's laws. Filial Wen emperor initially ascended, modest yielding not yet had time. Various statutes commands newly determined, and marquises all went to fiefs, these theories all originated from Jia Sheng. Thus Son of Heaven discussed appointing Jia Sheng to duke minister position. Jiang, Guan, Dongyang Marquis, Feng Jing and others all harmed him, thus slandered Jia Sheng saying: 'Luoyang person, young beginner scholar, specially wishes to monopolize power, disorder various affairs.' Thus Son of Heaven afterward also distanced him, did not use his proposals, appointed Jia Sheng as Changsha King tutor.

sentences.zh: 賈生既辭往行，聞長沙卑溼，自以壽不得長，又以適去，意不自得。

translations.literal: Jia Sheng already declined went, heard Changsha low wet, self thought longevity not long, moreover banished went, intention not self satisfied.
translations.idiomatic: Jia Sheng took his leave and departed. He had heard that Changsha was low and damp, and feared he would not live long there. Moreover, having been sent away in disgrace, his spirits were deeply low.

sentences.zh: 及渡湘水，為賦以弔屈原。

translations.literal: When crossed Xiang water, made rhapsody to lament Qu Yuan.
translations.idiomatic: As he crossed the Xiang River, he composed a rhapsody to mourn Qu Yuan.

sentences.zh: 其辭曰：

translations.literal: Its words said:
translations.idiomatic: Its words read:

translations.literal: Jia Sheng having declined went traveling, heard Changsha low wet, self-considered lifespan could not be long, moreover departing capital, intent not self-satisfied. When crossing Xiang water, composed rhapsody to mourn Qu Yuan. Its words say:

sentences.zh: 共承嘉惠兮，俟罪長沙。

translations.literal: Together receive fine favor, await crime Changsha.
translations.idiomatic: I have humbly received the emperor's gracious favor, yet now I await my punishment in Changsha.

sentences.zh: 側聞屈原兮，自沈汨羅。

translations.literal: Side heard Qu Yuan, self drowned Miluo.
translations.idiomatic: I have heard of Qu Yuan, who drowned himself in the Miluo.

sentences.zh: 造託湘流兮，敬弔先生。

translations.literal: Created entrusted Xiang flow, respectfully lament master.
translations.idiomatic: I entrust these words to the Xiang's current, to pay my respectful tribute to the master.

sentences.zh: 遭世罔極兮，乃隕厥身。

translations.literal: Encountered world without standard, then fell his body.
translations.idiomatic: He met a world without measure or principle, and so he let his body fall.

sentences.zh: 嗚呼哀哉，逢時不祥！

translations.literal: Alas sorrowful, met untimely time!
translations.idiomatic: Alas, how grievous! He was born into an inauspicious time!

sentences.zh: 鸞鳳伏竄兮，鴟梟翺翔：闒茸尊顯兮，讒諛得志；

translations.literal: Luan phoenix hide conceal, owl kite fly soar: base people honored exalted, slander flatterers get ambition;
translations.idiomatic: The phoenix and the luan hide and cower, while the owl and the kite soar freely: the lowly and worthless are honored and exalted, the slanderers and flatterers achieve their ambitions;

sentences.zh: 賢聖逆曳兮，方正倒植。

translations.literal: Worthy sages dragged backward, upright square inverted planted.
translations.idiomatic: the worthy and the wise are dragged backward, the upright and the honest are planted upside down.

sentences.zh: 世謂伯夷貪兮，謂盜跖廉；

translations.literal: World calls Bo Yi greedy, calls Dao Zhi frugal;
translations.idiomatic: The world calls Bo Yi greedy and Robber Zhi virtuous;

sentences.zh: 莫邪為頓兮，鉛刀為铦。

translations.literal: Mo Ye made blunt, lead knife made sharp.
translations.idiomatic: the sword Mo Ye is deemed blunt, while a knife of lead is called keen.

sentences.zh: 于嗟嚜嚜兮，生之無故！

translations.literal: Alas dark dark, life without reason!
translations.idiomatic: Alas, how dark, how dark — this life without reason!

sentences.zh: 斡棄周鼎兮寶康瓠，騰駕罷牛兮驂蹇驢，驥垂兩耳兮服鹽車。

translations.literal: Swirled abandoned Zhou tripod treasured calabash, mounted drove exhausted ox hitched lame donkey, steed hung two ears yoked salt cart.
translations.idiomatic: They cast aside the sacred Zhou tripod and treasure a hollow gourd; they harness an exhausted ox with a lame donkey for a team, while the fine steed droops its ears, yoked to a salt cart.

sentences.zh: 章甫薦屨兮，漸不可久；

translations.literal: Zhang Fu presented shoe, gradually cannot last;
translations.idiomatic: A noble cap is used to line a shoe — such an inversion cannot last;

sentences.zh: 嗟苦先生兮，獨離此咎！

translations.literal: Alas bitter master, alone separated this blame!
translations.idiomatic: Alas, poor Master — you alone bore the blame for this!

translations.literal: Together receive fine favor, await guilt Changsha. Side heard Qu Yuan, self-drowned Mi Luo. Made entrusted Xiang current, respectfully mourn master. Encountered age without extreme, thus fell his person. Alas sorrowful, encountered age inauspicious! Luan phoenix crouch hide, owl kite soar fly: despicable honored eminent, slanders obtain ambition; Worthy sages dragged backward, upright overturned planted. Age calls Bo Yi greedy, calls Dao Zhi honest; Mo Ye made blunt, lead knife made sharp. Alas dark dark, life without reason! Turned abandoned Zhou tripod treasured marrow gourd, galloped drove exhausted ox hitched lame donkey, steed drooped two ears harnessed salt cart. Ceremonial cap recommended shoes, gradually cannot last; Alas bitter master, alone separate this blame!

sentences.zh: 訊曰：已矣，國其莫我知，獨堙郁兮其誰語？

translations.literal: Message says: Finished, state none know me, alone choked pent up who speak to?
translations.idiomatic: The envoi says: It is over! None in the realm know me — I choke in solitary anguish, and to whom can I speak?

sentences.zh: 鳳漂漂其高遰兮，夫固自縮而遠去。

translations.literal: Phoenix floats floats its high far, husband indeed self contracted far gone.
translations.idiomatic: The phoenix drifts aloft and soars away — it withdraws of its own accord and departs into the distance.

sentences.zh: 襲九淵之神龍兮，沕深潛以自珍。

translations.literal: Followed nine abysses divine dragon, swam deep hidden self treasure.
translations.idiomatic: Like the divine dragon of the nine abysses, it dives deep and hides, treasuring itself in secret.

sentences.zh: 彌融爚以隱處兮，夫豈從螘與蛭螾？

translations.literal: Widely melted bright hidden place, husband how follow ants leeches worms?
translations.idiomatic: It shines with a radiance that conceals itself — how could it ever keep company with ants and leeches?

sentences.zh: 所貴聖人之神德兮，遠濁世而自藏。

translations.literal: What valued sage man divine virtue, far turbid world self hide.
translations.idiomatic: What we prize in the sage is his divine virtue — he withdraws far from the turbid world and conceals himself.

sentences.zh: 使騏驥可得系羈兮，豈云異夫犬羊！

translations.literal: Make steed can get tied restrained, how say different from dogs sheep!
translations.idiomatic: If a fine steed could be tethered and bridled like any beast, how would it differ from dogs and sheep!

sentences.zh: 般紛紛其離此尤兮，亦夫子之辜也！

translations.literal: Generally confused left this blame, also master guilt!
translations.idiomatic: That all this turmoil should descend upon you was also, in part, the Master's own doing!

sentences.zh: 瞝九州而相君兮，何必懷此都也？

translations.literal: Look nine provinces choose lord, why must cherish this capital?
translations.idiomatic: You could have looked across the Nine Provinces to find a worthy lord — why cling so stubbornly to this one capital?

sentences.zh: 鳳皇翔于千仞之上兮，覽德惪而下之；

translations.literal: Phoenix soars thousand ren above, views virtue solid descend it;
translations.idiomatic: The phoenix soars a thousand ren above, and only when it surveys true virtue below does it descend;

sentences.zh: 見細德之險（微）［徵］兮，搖增翮逝而去之。

translations.literal: See small virtue danger omen, shake increase wings departed gone.
translations.idiomatic: when it sees the slightest sign of faltering virtue, it beats its wings and soars away.

sentences.zh: 彼尋常之汙瀆兮，豈能容吞舟之魚！

translations.literal: That ordinary dirty ditch, how can contain swallow boat fish!
translations.idiomatic: How could an ordinary ditch ever contain a fish large enough to swallow a boat!

sentences.zh: 橫江湖之鱣鱏兮，固將制於蟻螻。

translations.literal: Horizontal rivers lakes sturgeon, indeed will controlled by ants mole crickets.
translations.idiomatic: The great sturgeon that rules the rivers and lakes will surely be mastered at last by ants and mole crickets.

translations.literal: Message says: Finished, state none know me, alone stifled depressed who speak? Phoenix drifting high far, indeed self-shrink far go. Follow nine abysses divine dragon, deep submerged self-treasure. Profoundly hidden concealed place, how follow ants leeches? Valued sages divine virtue, far muddy age self-hide. Make qi ji can obtain tied restrained, how different from dogs sheep! Various confused depart this fault, also master's guilt! Look nine provinces mutually lord, why must cherish this capital? Phoenix soars thousand ren above, views virtue solid descend; Sees petty virtue dangerous signs, shakes increased feathers departed gone. That common filth ditch, how can contain swallow boat fish! Horizontal rivers lakes giant fish, indeed will controlled by ants mole crickets.

sentences.zh: 賈生為長沙王太傅三年，有鸮飛入賈生舍，止于坐隅。

translations.literal: Jia Sheng was Changsha King Taifu three years, had owl fly enter Jia Sheng dwelling, stopped at seat corner.
translations.idiomatic: After Jia Sheng had served as Grand Tutor to the King of Changsha for three years, an owl flew into his lodgings and perched in the corner of his seat.

sentences.zh: 楚人命鸮曰「服」。

translations.literal: Chu people call owl 'Fu'.
translations.idiomatic: The people of Chu call the owl 'fu.'

sentences.zh: 賈生既以適居長沙，長沙卑溼，自以為壽不得長，傷悼之，乃為賦以自廣。

translations.literal: Jia Sheng already banished live Changsha, Changsha low wet, self thought longevity not long, wounded mourned it, then made rhapsody self console.
translations.idiomatic: Jia Sheng had been banished to live in Changsha, which was low and damp. Believing he would not live long, he grieved deeply and composed a rhapsody to console himself.

sentences.zh: 其辭曰：

translations.literal: Its words said:
translations.idiomatic: Its words read:

translations.literal: Jia Sheng as Changsha King tutor three years, owl flew into Jia Sheng residence, stopped at seat corner. Chu people name owl 'fu'. Jia Sheng since banished dwell Changsha, Changsha low wet, self-considered lifespan could not be long, injured mourned it, thus composed rhapsody self-consolation. Its words say:

sentences.zh: 單閼之歲兮，四月孟夏，庚子日施兮，服集予舍，止于坐隅，貌甚閒暇。

translations.literal: Single narrow year, fourth month first summer, Gengzi day loose, service gathered my dwelling, stopped at seat corner, appearance very leisurely.
translations.idiomatic: In the year of Shanhe, in the fourth month of early summer, on the day of Gengzi, an owl alighted on my dwelling and perched in the corner of my seat, looking perfectly at ease.

sentences.zh: 異物來集兮，私怪其故，發書占之兮，筴言其度。

translations.literal: Strange thing come gather, privately strange its reason, sent book divine it, bamboo spoke its measure.
translations.idiomatic: A strange creature had come to alight — in private, I wondered at the reason. I opened a book of divination to consult the oracle, and the bamboo slips spoke of its portent.

sentences.zh: 曰「野鳥入處兮，主人將去」。

translations.literal: Said 'wild bird enter dwelling, master will go'.
translations.idiomatic: They said: 'When a wild bird enters the dwelling, the master is about to depart.'

sentences.zh: 請問于服兮：「予去何之？

translations.literal: Please ask Fu: 'I go where?
translations.idiomatic: I asked the owl: 'Where shall I go?

sentences.zh: 吉乎告我，凶言其菑。

translations.literal: Auspicious tell me, inauspicious speak its calamity.
translations.idiomatic: If it is auspicious, tell me so; if ill-omened, speak of the calamity to come.

sentences.zh: 淹數之度兮，語予其期。」

translations.literal: Soak several measure, tell me its time.
translations.idiomatic: Reckon the span of my days, and tell me when the hour will come.'

sentences.zh: 服乃嘆息，舉首奮翼，口不能言，請對以意。

translations.literal: Fu then sighed, raised head shook wings, mouth cannot speak, please respond with intention.
translations.idiomatic: The owl sighed, raised its head, and beat its wings. Its mouth could not speak, but it seemed to answer with its intent.

translations.literal: Single gate year, fourth month first summer, geng zi day si, fu gathered my residence, stopped at seat corner, appearance very leisurely. Strange thing come gathered, privately strange its reason, opened book divined it, tally spoke its measure. Said 'wild bird enter dwelling, master will depart'. Request ask fu: 'I depart where go? Auspicious tell me, inauspicious speak its calamity. Dwell number measure, tell me its time.' Fu then sighed, raised head shook wings, mouth cannot speak, requested respond with intention.

sentences.zh: 萬物變化兮，固無休息。

translations.literal: Myriad things change, indeed no rest.
translations.idiomatic: The myriad things are ever changing — there is never any rest.

sentences.zh: 斡流而遷兮，或推而還。

translations.literal: Rotate flow migrate, or push return.
translations.idiomatic: They revolve and flow and shift; pushed forward, they circle back again.

sentences.zh: 形氣轉續兮，變化而嬗。

translations.literal: Form qi transfer continue, change transform.
translations.idiomatic: Form and breath pass on in ceaseless succession, changing and transforming without end.

sentences.zh: 沕穆無窮兮，胡可勝言！

translations.literal: Swim silent no end, how can exhaust speak!
translations.idiomatic: Boundless and unfathomable — how could words ever encompass it!

sentences.zh: 禍兮福所倚，福兮禍所伏；

translations.literal: Calamity fortune depend on each other, fortune calamity lie hidden;
translations.idiomatic: Misfortune — that is where fortune leans; fortune — that is where misfortune hides;

sentences.zh: 憂喜聚門兮，吉凶同域。

translations.literal: Worry joy gather gate, auspicious inauspicious same region.
translations.idiomatic: sorrow and joy gather at the same gate, the auspicious and the dire share the same ground.

sentences.zh: 彼吳彊大兮，夫差以敗；

translations.literal: That Wu strong great, Fu Chai with defeat;
translations.idiomatic: The state of Wu was mighty and strong, yet Fu Chai met defeat;

sentences.zh: 越棲會稽兮，句踐霸世。

translations.literal: Yue dwell Kuaiji, Gou Jian hegemon world.
translations.idiomatic: Yue cowered at Kuaiji, yet Goujian rose to dominate the age.

sentences.zh: 斯游遂成兮，卒被五刑；

translations.literal: Si You finally succeed, finally received five punishments;
translations.idiomatic: Li Si roamed and achieved success, yet in the end he suffered the Five Punishments;

sentences.zh: 傅說胥靡兮，乃相武丁。

translations.literal: Fu Yue slave labor, then became minister Wu Ding.
translations.idiomatic: Fu Yue toiled as a convict laborer, yet rose to become chief minister to King Wu Ding.

sentences.zh: 夫禍之與福兮，何異糾纆。

translations.literal: That calamity and fortune, what different twisted rope.
translations.idiomatic: Misfortune and fortune — how are they any different from the strands of a twisted rope?

sentences.zh: 命不可說兮，孰知其極？

translations.literal: Fate cannot explain, who knows its limit?
translations.idiomatic: Fate cannot be explained — who knows where it will end?

sentences.zh: 水激則旱兮，矢激則遠。

translations.literal: Water pressed then shoots high, arrow pressed then goes far.
translations.idiomatic: Water forced through a narrow channel shoots high; an arrow released from a drawn bow flies far.

sentences.zh: 萬物回薄兮，振蕩相轉。

translations.literal: Myriad things return press, shake sway transfer each other.
translations.idiomatic: The myriad things surge and press upon one another, buffeted and turning without cease.

sentences.zh: 雲蒸雨降兮，錯繆相紛。

translations.literal: Cloud steam rain fall, wrong error mix confused.
translations.idiomatic: Clouds rise as steam and rain descends; all is tangled and interwoven.

sentences.zh: 大專槃物兮，坱軋無垠。

translations.literal: Great concentrated encompass things, vast press no border.
translations.idiomatic: The great Way encompasses all things, vast and boundless, without limit.

sentences.zh: 天不可與慮兮，道不可與謀。

translations.literal: Heaven cannot with deliberate, way cannot with plan.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven cannot be reasoned with; the Way cannot be plotted against.

sentences.zh: 遲數有命兮，惡識其時？

translations.literal: Slow hasten have fate, how know its time?
translations.idiomatic: Whether slow or swift, all is fated — who can know the appointed hour?

translations.literal: Myriad things change, indeed no rest. Turn flow migrate, or push return. Form qi transfer continue, change transform. Profound silent no limit, how can exhaust speak! Calamity fortune relies on, fortune calamity hides; Worry joy gather gate, auspicious inauspicious same domain. That Wu strong great, Fu Cha with defeat; Yue dwelt Kui Ji, Gou Jian hegemon world. Si You succeeded, finally suffered five punishments; Fu Yue slave labor, then ministered Wu Ding. Calamity and fortune, what different rope tangle. Fate cannot explain, who knows its extreme? Water shoots then dry, arrow shoots then far. Myriad things turn press, shake move mutually transfer. Cloud steams rain descends, wrong error mutually confused. Great specialized turns things, vast presses no border. Heaven cannot with deliberate, way cannot with plan. Slow number has fate, evil know its time?

sentences.zh: 且夫天地為鑪兮，造化為工；

translations.literal: Moreover heaven earth furnace, creation worker;
translations.idiomatic: Heaven and earth are the furnace; creation itself is the smith;

sentences.zh: 陰陽為炭兮，萬物為銅。

translations.literal: Yin yang charcoal, myriad things copper.
translations.idiomatic: yin and yang are the charcoal; the myriad things are the bronze.

sentences.zh: 合散消息兮，安有常則；

translations.literal: Combine disperse grow decline, how have constant rule;
translations.idiomatic: Combining and dispersing, waxing and waning — where is there any constant law?

sentences.zh: 千變萬化兮，未始有極。

translations.literal: Thousand change ten thousand transform, never have limit.
translations.idiomatic: A thousand changes, ten thousand transformations — from the beginning, there has never been an end.

sentences.zh: 忽然為人兮，何足控摶；

translations.literal: Suddenly become person, what sufficient hold grasp;
translations.idiomatic: To become a human being in a sudden moment — what is there to cling to?

sentences.zh: 化為異物兮，又何足患！

translations.literal: Transform become different thing, again what sufficient worry!
translations.idiomatic: To be transformed into some other being — why should that be cause for grief!

sentences.zh: 小知自私兮，賤彼貴我；

translations.literal: Small knowledge self private, despise that value I;
translations.idiomatic: The small-minded are selfish, despising others and prizing themselves;

sentences.zh: 通人大觀兮，物無不可。

translations.literal: Penetrating great person view, things none not can.
translations.idiomatic: the man of broad understanding takes the great view, and to him nothing is unacceptable.

sentences.zh: 貪夫徇財兮，烈士徇名；

translations.literal: Greedy man follow wealth, fierce knight follow name;
translations.idiomatic: The greedy man dies for wealth; the man of fierce conviction dies for fame;

sentences.zh: 夸者死權兮，品庶馮生。

translations.literal: Boaster die power, common people rely life.
translations.idiomatic: the vainglorious die for power, while the common people simply cling to life.

sentences.zh: 述迫之徒兮，或趨西東；

translations.literal: State urge crowd, or hasten west east;
translations.idiomatic: Those driven by compulsion rush hither and thither, east and west;

sentences.zh: 大人不曲兮，億變齊同。

translations.literal: Great person not bend, hundred million change same same.
translations.idiomatic: but the great man does not bend — amid a hundred million changes, all is the same to him.

sentences.zh: 拘士系俗兮，攌如囚拘；

translations.literal: Constrain knight tie custom, tied like prisoner;
translations.idiomatic: The narrow-minded scholar is bound by convention, fettered like a prisoner in chains;

sentences.zh: 至人遺物兮，獨與道俱。

translations.literal: Perfect person abandon things, alone with way together.
translations.idiomatic: but the perfected man transcends the world of things and dwells alone with the Way.

sentences.zh: 眾人或或兮，好惡積意；

translations.literal: Crowd people various, good bad accumulate intention;
translations.idiomatic: The common crowd wavers this way and that, their likes and dislikes piling up within them;

sentences.zh: 真人淡漠兮，獨與道息。

translations.literal: True person indifferent, alone with way rest.
translations.idiomatic: but the true man is serene and detached, resting alone with the Way.

sentences.zh: 釋知遺形兮，超然自喪；

translations.literal: Release knowledge abandon form, transcend self forget;
translations.idiomatic: He releases knowledge and abandons form, transcending and forgetting himself;

sentences.zh: 寥廓忽荒兮，與道翺翔。

translations.literal: Vast suddenly vast, with way soar fly.
translations.idiomatic: in the vast and boundless emptiness, he soars with the Way.

sentences.zh: 乘流則逝兮，得坻則止；

translations.literal: Ride current then go, get shore then stop;
translations.idiomatic: He rides the current and goes where it takes him; when he reaches a bank, he stops;

sentences.zh: 縱軀委命兮，不私與己。

translations.literal: Free body entrust fate, not private with self.
translations.idiomatic: he releases his body and entrusts himself to fate, without a thought of self.

sentences.zh: 其生若浮兮，其死若休；

translations.literal: His life like float, his death like rest;
translations.idiomatic: His life is like drifting; his death is like rest;

sentences.zh: 澹乎若深淵之靜，氾乎若不系之舟。

translations.literal: Indifferent like deep abyss quiet, vast like not tied boat.
translations.idiomatic: he is tranquil as a still, deep pool, and unmoored as a boat adrift without a tether.

sentences.zh: 不以生故自寶兮，養空而浮；

translations.literal: Not because life reason self treasure, nurture empty float;
translations.idiomatic: He does not prize himself merely because he is alive, but nourishes emptiness and floats free;

sentences.zh: 德人無累兮，知命不憂。

translations.literal: Virtue person no burden, know fate not worry.
translations.idiomatic: the man of virtue bears no burdens — knowing his fate, he does not grieve.

sentences.zh: 細故遰葪兮，何足以疑！

translations.literal: Small matter far small, what sufficient doubt!
translations.idiomatic: Petty matters and trifling cares — what reason is there to be troubled by them!

translations.literal: Moreover heaven earth as furnace, creation as artisan; Yin yang as charcoal, myriad things as copper. Combine disperse wax wane, how have constant rule; Thousand changes ten thousand transformations, not begun have extreme. Suddenly become human, what sufficient control grasp; Transform become different thing, again what sufficient worry! Small knowledge self-private, belittle others value self; Greatly connected great view, things nothing cannot. Greedy man risk wealth, fierce scholars risk name; Boasters die for power, common people rely on life. Urged pressed followers, or hurry west east; Great man not bent, hundred million changes same together. Constrained scholar tied customs, entangled like prisoner restrained; Perfect man abandons things, alone with way together. Crowd confused confused, good evil accumulate intention; True person indifferent indifferent, alone with way rest. Release knowledge abandon form, transcend self-forget; Vast empty sudden wild, with way soar fly. Ride current then go, obtain shore then stop; Release body entrust fate, not private with self. His life like float, his death like rest; Indifferent like deep abyss quiet, broad like untied boat. Not because life self-treasure, nourish emptiness float; Virtue man no burden, know fate not worry. Small matters far distant, what sufficient doubt!

sentences.zh: 後歲餘，賈生徵見。

translations.literal: After year plus, Jia Sheng summoned appear.
translations.idiomatic: More than a year later, Jia Sheng was summoned back to court for an audience.

sentences.zh: 孝文帝方受釐，坐宣室。

translations.literal: Filial Wen Emperor just received ceremony, sat Xuanshi.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wen had just performed a sacrificial offering and was seated in the Xuanshi Hall.

sentences.zh: 上因感鬼神事，而問鬼神之本。

translations.literal: Emperor because sense ghost spirit matters, ask ghost spirit origin.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor, moved by thoughts of ghosts and spirits, asked about the origin and nature of the spiritual world.

sentences.zh: 賈生因具道所以然之狀。

translations.literal: Jia Sheng then completely explain so reason condition.
translations.idiomatic: Jia Sheng explained in full the reasons and principles behind these matters.

sentences.zh: 至夜半，文帝前席。

translations.literal: To midnight, Wen Emperor advance mat.
translations.idiomatic: By midnight, Emperor Wen had moved forward to the edge of his mat, leaning in to listen.

sentences.zh: 既罷，曰：「吾久不見賈生，自以為過之，今不及也。」

translations.literal: After finished, said: 'I long not see Jia Sheng, self think surpass him, now not equal.'
translations.idiomatic: When the audience was over, the emperor said: 'I have not seen Jia Sheng for a long time, and I thought I had surpassed him. Now I see I am not his equal.'

sentences.zh: 居頃之，拜賈生為梁懷王太傅。

translations.literal: Dwell moment, appoint Jia Sheng Liang Huai King Taifu.
translations.idiomatic: Shortly afterward, he appointed Jia Sheng as Grand Tutor to King Huai of Liang.

sentences.zh: 梁懷王，文帝之少子，愛，而好書，故令賈生傅之。

translations.literal: Liang Huai King, Wen Emperor young son, loved, and good books, therefore make Jia Sheng tutor him.
translations.idiomatic: King Huai of Liang was Emperor Wen's youngest son, whom the emperor loved dearly. The prince was fond of learning, and so the emperor appointed Jia Sheng as his tutor.

translations.literal: Later year plus, Jia Sheng summoned seen. Filial Wen emperor just received li, sat Xuanshi. Above thus sensed ghosts spirits affairs, asked ghosts spirits foundation. Jia Sheng thus completely spoke its so condition. To midnight, Wen emperor advanced mat. Having finished, said: 'I long not see Jia Sheng, self-considered surpassed him, now cannot match.' Dwelt moment, appointed Jia Sheng as Liang Huai King tutor. Liang Huai King, Wen emperor young son, loved, and liked books, thus commanded Jia Sheng tutor him.

sentences.zh: 文帝復封淮南厲王子四人皆為列侯。

translations.literal: Wen Emperor again enfeoff four sons of Huainan Li King all as marquises.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wen once again enfeoffed all four sons of the cruel King of Huainan as marquises.

sentences.zh: 賈生諫，以為患之興自此起矣。

translations.literal: Jia Sheng admonished, thought calamity rise from this.
translations.idiomatic: Jia Sheng remonstrated, warning that calamity would arise from this.

sentences.zh: 賈生數上疏，言諸侯或連數郡，非古之制，可稍削之。

translations.literal: Jia Sheng several times submit memorials, speak marquises some connect several commanderies, not ancient system, can gradually reduce them.
translations.idiomatic: Jia Sheng submitted memorials on multiple occasions, arguing that some of the feudal lords controlled territories spanning several commanderies, which was contrary to the ancient system, and that their domains should be gradually reduced.

sentences.zh: 文帝不聽。

translations.literal: Wen Emperor not listen.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wen did not heed him.

translations.literal: Wen emperor again enfeoffed Huainan Li Wang sons four people all as marquises. Jia Sheng advised, considered calamity rise from this. Jia Sheng several times submitted memorials, spoke various marquises connected several commanderies, not ancient system, can gradually reduce them. Wen emperor not listened.

sentences.zh: 居數年，懷王騎，墮馬而死，無後。

translations.literal: Dwell several years, Huai King ride horse, fall horse die, no posterity.
translations.idiomatic: Several years later, King Huai went riding, fell from his horse, and died. He left no heir.

sentences.zh: 賈生自傷為傅無狀，哭泣歲餘，亦死。

translations.literal: Jia Sheng self blame as tutor without achievement, cry weep year plus, also die.
translations.idiomatic: Jia Sheng blamed himself bitterly for having failed as a tutor. He wept for more than a year, and then he too died.

sentences.zh: 賈生之死時年三十三矣。

translations.literal: Jia Sheng death time age thirty three.
translations.idiomatic: At the time of his death, Jia Sheng was only thirty-three years old.

sentences.zh: 及孝文崩，孝武皇帝立，舉賈生之孫二人至郡守，而賈嘉最好學，世其家，與余通書。

translations.literal: When Filial Wen passed, Filial Wu Emperor established, raise Jia Sheng two grandsons to commandery governors, Jia Jia most good learning, inherit family, with me communicate writings.

sentences.zh: 至孝昭時，列為九卿。

translations.literal: To Filial Zhao time, listed as nine ministers.

Dwelt several years, Huai Wang rode, fell horse died, no posterity. Jia Sheng self-injured as tutor without achievement, wept year plus, also died. Jia Sheng death time age thirty-three. When Filial Wen collapsed, Filial Wu emperor ascended, promoted Jia Sheng grandsons two to commandery governors, Jia Jia most liked learning, inherited family, with me exchanged letters. To Filial Zhao time, listed as nine ministers.

sentences.zh: 太史公曰：余讀離騷、天問、招魂、哀郢，悲其志。

translations.literal: Grand Historian said: I read Li Sao, Tian Wen, Zhao Hun, Ai Ying, grieve his ambition.

sentences.zh: 適長沙，觀屈原所自沈淵，未嘗不垂涕，想見其為人。

translations.literal: Went Changsha, view Qu Yuan where self drowned abyss, never not shed tears, imagine see his person.

sentences.zh: 及見賈生弔之，又怪屈原以彼其材，游諸侯，何國不容，而自令若是。

translations.literal: When see Jia Sheng lament him, again strange Qu Yuan with his talent, travel various lords, what country not contain, yet self make like this.

sentences.zh: 讀服烏賦，同死生，輕去就，又爽然自失矣。

translations.literal: Read Fu Ou assignment, same death life, light leave take, again clearly self lost.

Grand Historian says: I read 'Li Sao', 'Tian Wen', 'Zhao Hun', 'Ai Ying', sorrowful his ambition. Passed Changsha, viewed Qu Yuan self-drowned abyss, never not shed tears, imagined saw his person. When saw Jia Sheng mourn him, again strange Qu Yuan with his talent, traveled feudal lords, what state not contain, yet self made like this. Read 'Fu Niao' rhapsody, same death life, light depart stay, again clearly self-lost.
